extra.message: User-controlled 'id' query parameter is concatenated directly into a SQL query via string concatenation (+ operator) passed to cursor.execute(). An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL by manipulating the id parameter (e.g., ?id=2 UNION ALL SELECT NULL,NULL,NULL,NULL).

User-controlled 'comment' query parameter is injected into an SQL INSERT statement via Python string formatting (% operator). An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL by including single quotes or SQL metacharacters in the comment parameter, leading to data corruption or unauthorized data access.

User-controlled 'password' query parameter is concatenated directly into a SQL SELECT query. While the 'username' parameter is sanitized with re.sub(r'[^\w]', ''), the 'password' parameter is inserted unsanitized. An attacker can perform SQL injection via the password field (e.g., password=' OR '1'='1) to bypass authentication entirely.

User-controlled 'domain' query parameter is concatenated directly into a shell command string passed to subprocess.run() with shell=True. An attacker can inject arbitrary OS commands via the domain parameter (e.g., ?domain=localhost;id). This allows full remote command execution on the server.

User-controlled 'path' query parameter is passed to open() with 'rb' mode after os.path.abspath() resolution. Although os.path.abspath() resolves relative paths, it does not prevent directory traversal (e.g., ?path=/etc/passwd or ?path=../../../etc/passwd). An attacker can read arbitrary files from the server filesystem.

User-controlled file content (fetched via 'include' parameter, line 56) is passed directly to Python's exec() function. An attacker can either read an arbitrary local file (path traversal) or fetch a remote URL (SSRF) whose contents will be executed as Python code. This enables full remote code execution with arbitrary Python commands.

User-controlled 'include' query parameter is passed to open() without any path validation. An attacker can read arbitrary files by manipulating the include parameter (e.g., ?include=/etc/passwd), and the file contents are then executed via exec() on line 57.

User-controlled 'object' query parameter is deserialized using pickle.loads(). Pickle deserialization of untrusted data is inherently unsafe and can execute arbitrary code during the deserialization process. An attacker can craft a malicious pickle payload to achieve remote code execution.

User-controlled 'path' query parameter is passed to urllib.request.urlopen() when the value contains '://'. An attacker can use this to make the server send requests to arbitrary internal or external hosts (SSRF), potentially accessing internal services like cloud metadata endpoints (e.g., ?path=http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/).

User-controlled 'include' query parameter is passed to urllib.request.urlopen() when the value contains '://'. An attacker can use this SSRF vulnerability to make the server fetch remote URLs, and the fetched content is then executed via exec() on line 57, enabling both SSRF and remote code execution.

lxml XMLParser is configured with load_dtd=True, resolve_entities=True, and no_network=False, all of which enable XML External Entity (XXE) processing. An attacker can submit a malicious XML payload via the 'xml' parameter that defines external entities to read local files (e.g., file:///etc/passwd) or perform SSRF via HTTP URLs.

cwe: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

extra.message: User-controlled 'name' query parameter is inserted directly into an XPath query string via Python string formatting (% operator). An attacker can inject XPath expressions (e.g., ?name=' or '1'='1) to bypass the intended query logic and extract data for any user, including information not normally accessible.

User-controlled 'redir' query parameter is inserted directly into a meta refresh tag without any validation or sanitization. An attacker can use this open redirect to redirect users to arbitrary external URLs (e.g., ?redir=https://phishing-site.com), enabling phishing attacks.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site (Open Redirect)

extra.message: User-controlled 'v' query parameter is inserted directly into the HTML response via re.sub replacement without any HTML encoding or sanitization. An attacker can inject arbitrary JavaScript by including script tags in the 'v' parameter (e.g., ?v=<script>alert(1)</script>), resulting in reflected cross-site scripting.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation (Cross-site Scripting)

extra.message: User-submitted comments are stored in the database (line 50) and later retrieved and rendered directly into HTML via '%s' without any HTML encoding. An attacker can submit a comment containing malicious JavaScript (e.g., ) that will execute in the browsers of all users viewing the comments page.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation (Cross-site Scripting)

extra.message: User-controlled 'callback' query parameter is inserted directly into a JavaScript function call wrapper without any sanitization. An attacker can inject arbitrary JavaScript by supplying a callback value containing script code (e.g., ?callback=alert(1)). This enables JSONP-based reflected XSS attacks.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation (Cross-site Scripting)

extra.message: User-controlled 'charset' query parameter is inserted into the Content-Type header without validation. An attacker can set the charset to a value like 'utf-7' and construct UTF-7 encoded XSS payloads that bypass browser XSS filters, enabling cross-site scripting attacks.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation (Cross-site Scripting)

extra.message: User-controlled 'size' query parameter is used to allocate a string of size size*size characters (e.g., size=100000 would attempt to create a 10-billion-character string). An attacker can cause a denial of service by submitting a large size value, exhausting server memory and CPU.

Full Python stack traces are returned to the client via traceback.format_exc() when exceptions occur. This exposes internal implementation details, file paths, and source code snippets to attackers, aiding in further exploitation.

cwe: CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information

extra.message: The X-XSS-Protection header is explicitly set to '0', which disables the browser's built-in reflected XSS filter. This reduces the security of all users visiting the application, making XSS attacks more likely to succeed.

Hardcoded user credentials including the admin password '7en8aiDoh!' and three additional user passwords ('12345', 'gandalf', 'phest1945') are embedded in the source code as part of USERS_XML. This exposes all application credentials to anyone with access to the source code.

Session cookie (SESSIONID) is set via a meta refresh tag with neither the HttpOnly nor Secure flags. The cookie is accessible to JavaScript (enabling XSS-based session theft) and transmitted over unencrypted HTTP (enabling network-based session interception).
